whats_new.title: What's new — 13 July 2026
whats_new.lead_html: This update makes your dashboards more complete and more accurate. Data now flows and is attributed to the right people across Git, Jira, collaboration and Zoom, and several metrics that were showing wrong or empty values have been corrected. No new screens this time — everything below improves what you already use.
whats_new.in_short_html: In short: more of your team's activity now shows up correctly — Bitbucket reviews, Jira task delivery, collaboration and Zoom meetings are back and attributed properly, long-range Git charts are readable, and AI cost is shown as real money.

direct_reports_toggle.title: “Direct reports only” toggle is back
direct_reports_toggle.description_html: Filter a team down to just your direct reports — members, heatmap and metrics all update together. On by default, and hidden automatically for teams without sub-teams, where it had no effect.

member_expand_full_metrics.title: Full metrics when you expand a team member
member_expand_full_metrics.description_html: Expanding a person now opens their complete metric set instead of a trimmed subset — so you can assess someone without switching screens.

bitbucket_prs.title: Bitbucket pull requests now counted
bitbucket_prs.description_html: Pull-request activity from Bitbucket is attributed to the right person. Review metrics that used to show zero now reflect real work.

consistent_git_metrics.title: Consistent commit & lines-of-code metrics
consistent_git_metrics.description_html: Commit and LOC figures now come from a single shared source, so Git activity is reported the same way across connectors.

readable_git_charts.title: Readable quarterly & yearly Git charts
readable_git_charts.description_html: Long-range charts no longer repeat the same label over and over. Quarterly and annual trends are now legible.

jira_task_delivery.title: Jira Task Delivery metrics now populate
jira_task_delivery.description_html: Closed tasks are detected by their status category rather than English-only status names, so non-English and custom “Done” workflows report correctly.

zoom_data.title: Zoom meeting data restored
zoom_data.description_html: The Zoom sync was hitting an API limit and stalled for about ten days. It's fixed, so meeting activity repopulates going forward.

ai_adoption_graphs.title: AI adoption graphs fixed
ai_adoption_graphs.description_html: AI-adoption graphs now show real distributions with honest “no data” states instead of blanks or misleading zeros.

claude_code_cost.title: Claude Code cost shown as currency
claude_code_cost.description_html: The “Claude Code Cost” metric now displays as money instead of a raw number of cents — no more figures that looked inflated.

items.identity: People without a matching email aren't attributed yet — this can still leave some Bitbucket and Jira metrics incomplete. Identity resolution is in progress.
items.gitlab_loc: GitLab lines-of-code still shows 0. The commit and LOC fix above applies elsewhere; GitLab LOC is being worked on.
items.jira_kpis: Some Jira KPIs may still be blank or slow to load on freshly set-up environments.

metrics_engine.title: One accurate metrics engine
metrics_engine.description: Bringing all metric families onto a single engine, with correct “no data” states in place of misleading zeros everywhere.

people_matching.title: Better people matching
people_matching.description: Attributing work to the right person even across multiple or missing email addresses — the root cause behind the gaps above.

mock_banner.title: Synthetic data
mock_banner.description_html: UI is rendering using mock data.

members_table.click_hint: Click member to open IC dashboard

team_metrics_modal.title: All Team Member Metrics
team_metrics_modal.empty: No metrics returned for these members.
